Notice Of Motion For Referees Final Report Of Sale Of Real Property Located At 233-235 Lincoln Way Pursuant To Interlocutory Judgment For Partition Dated 4-21-2022; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Approval Of Referee Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Nov-09-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via ZOOM
Benjamin C. Graves, Esq., LAW OFFICES OF BENJAMIN C. GRAVES, (415) 453-3600, benjamin@marinlitigation.com, for Plaintiffs Juliana Brodsky and Seth Rosenblatt
Amy Harrington, Esq., Harrington Law, PC, 415-558-7700, Court Appointed Partition Referee in Pro Per,

Hearing held. Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

OTHER AMY HARRINGTON'S Motion For Referees Final Report Of Sale Of Real Property Located At 233-235 Lincoln Way Pursuant To Interlocutory Judgment For Partition Dated 4-21-2022; Approval Of Referee Fees And Costs is GRANTED is as requested. No Overbidders. Final sale amount $1,350,000.00. Order is signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: W. Trupek, Sec Not Reported (D501)

OTHER AMY HARRINGTON'S Motion To Accept Referees Report On Manner Of Partition And Feasibility Of Physical Partition For Method Of Partition is granted. Referee Amy Harrington to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

PLAINTIFFS JULIANA BRODSKY AND SETH ROSENBLATT'S Joinder Of Motion To Accept Referees Report On Manner Of Partition And Feasibility Of Physical Partition For Method Of Partition is granted. Motion For Order Appointing Referee
Off Calendar Apr-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER APPOINTING REFEREE is GRANTED, in part. Amy Harrington is appointed referee. However, the scope of her duties is limited to assisting the Court in determining the manner of partition, specifically whether physical partition is feasible.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Interocutory Judgment Of Partition By Sale
Off Calendar Apr-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF PARTITION BY SALE is GRANTED, in part. The manner of partition shall be determined at a later date after the Court receives a recommendation from the referee.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
